etymology.description: semantic 穴 (穴) (xué, "cave, hole") + phonetic 工 (工) (gōng) = hollow space -> empty
etymology.interpretation: The character 空(kōng, "empty") combines 穴 (穴) (xué, "cave, hole") as the semantic component, indicating a hollow or vacant area, with 工 (工) (gōng) serving as the phonetic component. This phono-semantic compound suggests the concept of a space that is hollow and without content, hence "empty." The abstract idea of vacancy is derived from the literal representation of a void or cavity.

examples.chinese: 这个房间是空的(這個房間是空的)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè gè fángjiān shì kōng de.

examples.chinese: 他感到内心空虚(他感到內心空虛)。
早晨的街道很空(早晨的街道很空)，没有人(沒有人)。
examples.pinyin: Zǎochén de jiēdào hěn kōng, méiyǒu rén.
examples.english: The streets are empty in the morning, with nobody around.
